title: Adopt Server-Backed Agent Runtimes
description: Adopt server-backed per-task execution for supported coding agents. Make codex use codex app-server and opencode use opencode serve by default, introduce a shared task-session runtime above backend-specific protocols, preserve explicit legacy fallbacks during migration, and capture the architecture in an ADR.

title: Write ADR-002 server-backed agent runtimes
description: Document the decision to move default codex execution to codex app-server and default opencode execution to opencode serve, using a shared task-session runtime above protocol-specific drivers while preserving explicit legacy fallbacks during migration.

title: Adopt Codex app-server as default backend
description: Implement a codex app-server driver over stdio JSON-RPC, use one runner-owned ephemeral thread and turn per task, normalize Codex events into runner progress, and keep codex CLI available as an explicit fallback backend.

title: Implement Codex app-server stdio JSON-RPC driver
description: Start codex app-server per task over stdio, perform initialize and initialized handshake, create one runner-owned ephemeral thread and turn, send the task prompt, and stop the process when the task ends.

title: Map Codex notifications and completion into runner progress
description: Translate Codex app-server thread, turn, item, tool, and completion notifications into the shared normalized event model and runner progress updates, including review-mode completion handling.

title: Integrate codex backend selection and preserve codex-cli fallback
description: Wire yolo-agent and related entrypoints so the default codex backend resolves to the app-server driver while codex-cli remains available as an explicit legacy fallback backend.

title: Add tests for the Codex app-server runner
description: Add unit and integration coverage for Codex app-server startup, handshake, event streaming, cancellation, teardown, and fallback selection using the shared test harnesses.

title: Cut over defaults, docs, and tests
description: Switch built-in codex and opencode backends to the new server-backed defaults, preserve explicit legacy fallback backends, update docs and configuration examples, and add end-to-end coverage across yolo-agent and worker entrypoints.

title: Update built-in backend definitions and config examples
description: Change built-in codex and opencode catalog entries and example configuration so the default backends resolve to the new server-backed runtimes while explicit legacy fallback entries remain available.

title: Wire yolo-agent and linear worker to the new adapters
description: Update runner construction paths in yolo-agent and worker entrypoints so backend selection resolves to the shared runtime-backed Codex and OpenCode adapters by default without breaking explicit fallback backends.

title: Update docs and migration guidance for server-backed defaults
description: Document the new default execution model, backend names, fallback behavior, and operational notes for per-task process startup, readiness, cancellation, and teardown.

title: Add end-to-end coverage for default server-backed paths
description: Add end-to-end tests that exercise the default codex and opencode server-backed paths through the main runner entrypoints and verify teardown, progress reporting, and result classification behavior.

title: Converge ACP on shared runtime
description: Refactor ACP execution to reuse the shared task-session runtime so ACP inherits the same lifecycle supervision, event normalization, approval handling, timeout behavior, logging, and teardown semantics as the new server-backed backends.

title: Refactor ACP runner onto shared task-session lifecycle
description: Move ACP execution onto the shared runtime so ACP startup, cancellation, timeout handling, teardown, and artifact collection behave the same way as the new server-backed backends.

title: Normalize ACP approvals, questions, and events through shared runtime
description: Map ACP-specific permission requests, question flows, progress events, and completion signals into the shared normalized event model so higher-level runner code stays transport-agnostic.

title: Add regression coverage for ACP fallback behavior
description: Add tests that preserve current ACP fallback behavior while the new server-backed defaults land, including cancellation, permissions, logging, and result classification semantics.

title: Build shared task-session runtime
description: Introduce a shared runtime layer that owns per-task process lifecycle, readiness checks, execution control, normalized event streaming, approval/question handling, cancellation, teardown, logging, and artifacts for server-backed and protocol-backed agents.

title: Define common task-session runtime interfaces and event model
description: Define the shared runtime contracts for process lifecycle, execution control, normalized progress events, approvals, questions, cancellation, teardown, logging, and artifacts so Codex app-server, OpenCode serve, and ACP can plug into one execution framework.

title: Implement managed child-process supervisor and teardown policy
description: Implement the process supervisor that starts one backend process per task, waits for readiness, applies timeouts, performs graceful shutdown, escalates to forced kill when needed, and guarantees cleanup on completion, failure, or cancellation.

title: Implement shared readiness, cancellation, and logging helpers
description: Add reusable helpers for stdio and HTTP readiness checks, cancellation propagation, artifact assembly, normalized event emission, and backend stderr or protocol trace logging.

title: Add protocol test harnesses for stdio JSON-RPC and HTTP/SSE
description: Create reusable fake protocol harnesses so runtime and backend tests can simulate Codex stdio JSON-RPC flows, OpenCode HTTP and SSE flows, and future ACP convergence without requiring real agent binaries.

title: Adopt OpenCode serve as default backend
description: Implement an opencode serve driver over loopback HTTP and SSE, use one runner-owned ephemeral task session per task, normalize OpenCode events into runner progress, and keep ACP as an explicit fallback backend during migration.

title: Implement OpenCode serve process driver and health checks
description: Start opencode serve per task on loopback, allocate and manage ports safely, wait for global health readiness, and guarantee cleanup of the process and any ephemeral session state when the task finishes.

title: Implement OpenCode session, message, SSE, and approval flow
description: Create one runner-owned ephemeral OpenCode session per task, submit the task prompt, consume HTTP and SSE events, respond to permission or question flows programmatically, and determine completion reliably.

title: Integrate opencode backend selection and preserve opencode-acp fallback
description: Wire yolo-agent and related entrypoints so the default opencode backend resolves to the serve driver while opencode-acp remains available as an explicit legacy fallback backend during migration.

title: Add tests for the OpenCode serve runner
description: Add unit and integration coverage for OpenCode serve startup, health checks, event streaming, approvals, cancellation, teardown, and fallback selection using the shared test harnesses.
